game_name: Last Battle: Legend of the Final Hero

danger_signals.visual_form: Hostile characters blocking the player's path on screen
Timer display in lower-right corner of screen
danger_signals.meaning: Resource pressure; gradual health drain if time expires

danger_signals.visual_form: One-on-one arena battles with distinctive enemy characters
Non-linear environment layouts with multiple paths
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental hazards and traps present; navigation risk

danger_signals.visual_form: Enemy head explosions and bloody death animations
danger_signals.meaning: Extreme violence; post-apocalyptic brutality of world state

safety_signals.meaning: Temporary power-ups; offensive or defensive strength increase

safety_signals.visual_form: Visual change when power-up meter reaches threshold
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary invulnerability window; offensive dominance period

safety_signals.visual_form: Strategic map display after level completion
safety_signals.meaning: Safe transition point; player agency in path selection

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Martial artist with distinctive fighting stance
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Central focus character in all combat scenarios

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Recolored enemy variants (mutants vs. humans in Western version)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Three Rashō members rewritten as rebel generals with distinct appearances
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Distributed throughout levels as primary opposition

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Distinct sidekick and NPC character designs
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Appear as quest-givers and power-up providers within levels

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enemy designs shift to mutant appearance; gore and brutality in animations
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies attack relentlessly; hostile environment requires constant combat

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Empire collapse and revenge plot (narrative state)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Three antagonistic generals appear as boss encounters; varying level designs
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Staged boss battles; players must complete levels in specific order to advance plot

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Power-up meter filling visually as enemies are defeated
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Transformation into super-powered state; rapid attack sequences become available

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Time limit active; graphic violence in enemy deaths (Japanese version)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Constant enemy spawning; pressure to complete objectives before time expires

environmental_storytelling.example: Linear side-scrolling segments establish protagonist's forward journey; dungeon mazes introduce environmental complexity
Western localization removes gore and changes character names (Kenshiro→Aarzak, Falco→Duke), downplaying post-apocalyptic aesthetic while maintaining plot structure
Post-level maps display current location and available paths, communicating geographic progression and narrative choice
Distinct antagonist encounters (Duke/Falco, three rebel generals) visually represent faction hierarchy and revenge plot escalation
Allied characters scattered throughout world provide contextual support, suggesting civilization remnants and social structure

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Attacks with punches and kicks from standing, jumping, and crouching positions; walks forward through levels
Performs rapid punches and kicks; increased offensive capability maintained for remainder of stage
Block player's forward path; wait for player approach
Attack player with melee strikes; are defeated and removed from level (fly offscreen in Western version; explode in Japanese version)
Engage in prolonged one-on-one combat with unique attack patterns; represent narrative conflict climax
Provide temporary stat increases (offensive/defensive strength) or health replenishment; do not engage in direct combat

notes: Last Battle (Japanese: Hokuto no Ken: Shinseikimatsu Kyūseishu Densetsu) is a licensed adaptation of Fist of the North Star manga/anime. Western localization removed gore, changed character names, and altered enemy designs to reduce post-apocalyptic aesthetic. The game communicates world state primarily through visual enemy design variants (mutants vs. humans), NPC presence signaling civilization structure, time pressure mechanics indicating resource scarcity, and boss escalation indicating faction conflict. The map system serves as both navigation aid and narrative progression indicator. Core danger signals rely on enemy presence and visual distinctiveness rather than environmental hazards or elaborate visual language.
